The King’s Speech (2010)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $138,797,449Details
International Box Office $291,263,764
Worldwide Box Office $430,061,213
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $34,199,829
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $7,824,920
Total $42,024,749
Further financial details...

Synopsis

After the death of his father King George V and the scandalous abdication of King Edward VIII, Bertie, who has suffered from a debilitating speech impediment all his life, is suddenly crowned King George VI of England. With his country on the brink of war and in desperate need of a leader, his wife, Elizabeth, the future Queen Mother, arranges for her husband to see an eccentric speech therapist, Lionel Logue. After a rough start, the two delve into an unorthodox course of treatment and eventually form an unbreakable bond.

Theater counts:4 opening theaters/2,584 max. theaters, 10.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $209,468,995

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 26th, 2010 (Limited) by Weinstein Co.
December 25th, 2010 (Expands Wide) by Weinstein Co.
April 1st, 2011 (Wide) by Weinstein Co. (PG-13 Version)
International Releases: January 7th, 2011 (Wide), released as The King's Speech (United Kingdom)
January 20th, 2011 (Limited) (New Zealand)
February 4th, 2021 (Limited) (Australia)
May 29th, 2025 (Limited) (New Zealand)
Video Release: April 19th, 2011 by Weinstein Co./Anchor Bay
MPA Rating: R for some language.
(Rating bulletin 2138, 9/15/2010)
PG-13 for language.
(Rating bulletin 2163, 3/16/2011)
Running Time: 118 minutes
Award nomination: 2011 Oscars Best Picture Nominee
Award win: BAFTA Best Film Winner, BAFTA Best Original Screenplay Winner, Directors Guild of America Outstanding Directorial Achievement in Theatrical Feature Film Winner, Oscars Best Picture Winner, Producers Guild of America Best Theatrical Motion Picture Winner, Screen Actors Guild Outstanding Performance by a Cast in a Motion Picture Winner
Cast, crew, or production detail: Voiceover/Narration
Geographical setting: England, Germany
Plot point: Dysfunctional Family, Medical and Hospitals, Royalty
Social setting: World War II
Subgenre: Historical Drama
Time period setting: 1930s
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: UK Film Council, See-Saw Films, Bedlam, Momentum Pictures, Aegis Film Fund, Molinare, FilmNation Entertainment, Weinstein Company
Production Countries: United Kingdom
Languages: English

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.


  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

After the death of his father King George V and the scandalous abdication of King Edward VIII, Bertie, who has suffered from a debilitating speech impediment all his life, is suddenly crowned King George VI of England. With his country on the brink of war and in desperate need of a leader, his wife, Elizabeth, the future Queen Mother, arranges for her husband to see an eccentric speech therapist, Lionel Logue. After a rough start, the two delve into an unorthodox course of treatment and eventually form an unbreakable bond.
